name: HTS_SCHED_FULL_VALIDATIONS
description: This table embodies the schedule validation execution and acts as a parent/root for the KPIs and schedule violation detailed information.

columns.Name: SCHED_FULL_VALIDATION_ID
columns.Comments: The ID of the schedule validation execution.

columns.Name: SCHEDULE_ID
columns.Comments: The ID of the schedule the validation was run on.

columns.Name: PERIOD_START_DATE
columns.Comments: The start date of the schedule period.

columns.Name: PERIOD_END_DATE
columns.Comments: The end date of the schedule period.

columns.Name: SCHEDULE_UNIT_ID
columns.Comments: The ID of the schedule unit associated with the validated schedule.

columns.Name: ORIGIN_LOOKUP_TYPE
columns.Comments: The lookup type denoting the validation origin enumeration.

columns.Name: ORIGIN_LOOKUP_CODE
columns.Comments: The lookup code denoting the validation origin enumeration value.

columns.Name: VALIDATION_INSTANT
columns.Comments: The moment the validation was executed.

columns.Name: JOB_REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: The identifier of the ESS full schedule validation job associated with the run information.

columns.Name: VALIDATION_PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: The identifier of the top level logical process embodying the full validation.

columns.Name: LAST_VALIDATION_FLAG
columns.Comments: A flag indicating if the full validation is the last one or not.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
